evidence: We have made, and are continuing to make, significant investments in AI including generative AI and agentic AI, to, among other things, develop new products or features for our existing products
increased investment in technology, particularly our cloud initiatives and software licensing
expect to incur additional costs in these areas in an effort to accelerate efficiencies through location strategy and enhanced AI capabilities

Nasdaq Verafin has leveraged AI for more than 20 years...Our innovative AI-based Targeted Typology Analytics solution examines a range of behavioral, transactional, third-party, and consortium insights
in our AxiomSL offering we are embedding advanced AI capabilities, from generative AI assistants to machine-learning analytics to enhance user productivity, predictive insights
New enhancements include generative AI tools that are designed to streamline alert triage and investigative workflows, supporting improved efficiency and reduced false positives

our AI-related product initiatives and offerings...may give rise to risks related to harmful content, accuracy, bias, discrimination, autonomous decision-making or action, IP infringement...data privacy, and cybersecurity

As we integrate third-party AI models...we face risks in how such third-party AI models were developed and deployed, including situations in which the third-party may lack a proper license or consent for the training data

the use of AI by bad actors, including the use of such tools to engage in social engineering or similar activities...could damage our reputation and result in: a loss of customers...significant liabilities, litigation or regulatory fines

limitations: ai_investment_intensity: No quantified dollar amounts or percentages specifically tied to AI investment are disclosed in the excerpts; spend references are qualitative or bundled with broader technology/cloud costs, capping score at 3.

ai_narrative_concreteness: Multiple named, deployed use cases described with operational detail (Verafin, AxiomSL, Calypso, surveillance), but no quantified business results (e.g., % reduction in false positives, dollar savings) are provided, capping score at 4.
capex_mentions: No specific dollar or quantity figures tied exclusively to AI/compute/data-center AI investment appear in the excerpts; the $140M restructuring charge is not AI-specific capex.
